commands.review_summary:
The PR changes Memory Wiki ingest to add canonical source-path hash suffixes for new source page IDs and filenames, preserve legacy local-file source pages on re-ingest, and add focused tests plus a changelog entry.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection rather than an executed repro: current main derives both source `pageId` and `sources/<slug>.md` from the same normalized title, so same-slug inputs target the same page. I did not run a local repro because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: Needs contributor real behavior proof, a current-main refresh, and maintainer acceptance of the source identity compatibility change; this is not a safe repair-job candidate yet.

target.title: fix(memory-wiki): prevent ingested source page collisions
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #65965.

This PR prevents ingested memory-wiki source pages from silently overwriting each other when different source files normalize to the same readable slug.

Previously, `ingestMemoryWikiSource(...)` derived both `pageId` and `pageRelativePath` directly from the slugified title. Different files could therefore collapse onto the same `source.<slug>` / `sources/<slug>.md` identity, especially with non-ASCII titles or repeated fallback-heavy names.

This keeps source page names readable, but adds a stable source-path-derived suffix for new source page identities.

## What changed

- keep the readable slug prefix for ingested source pages
- add a stable 12-hex hash suffix derived from the canonical source path for new source identities
- use the same resolved identity consistently for both:
  - `pageId`
  - `pageRelativePath`
- preserve legacy `source.<slug>` identities when re-ingesting existing vaults
- canonicalize source paths for identity matching, including symlink aliases where possible
- derive fallback titles from the user-provided path so symlinked inputs keep the expected user-facing name
- limit the compatibility lookup to source pages rather than scanning every queryable wiki page

## Regression coverage

Added focused coverage for:

- pure CJK titles no longer collapsing to the same source page
- similar titles with the same slug no longer overwriting each other
- repeated ingest of the same source remaining stable
- plain ASCII titles remaining readable and stable
- identical titles with different source paths remaining distinct
- existing legacy source pages being reused on re-ingest
- symlinked source paths resolving to the same source identity
- fallback titles using the user-provided input path rather than the canonical target filename
- compatibility lookup scanning only source pages

## Testing

Passed locally:

- `pnpm vitest run extensions/memory-wiki/src/ingest.test.ts`
- `node scripts/test-projects.mjs extensions/memory-wiki`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 extensions/memory-wiki/src/ingest.ts extensions/memory-wiki/src/ingest.test.ts`

GitHub Actions are green on the latest head commit.

## Scope

This PR intentionally stays focused on preventing source page identity collisions.

It does not redesign the broader wiki identity system, and it does not attempt to migrate or repair historical collisions already present in existing vaults.

One remaining performance trade-off is that ingest still performs a source-page compatibility lookup before compiling the vault. This is now limited to `sources/*.md`, not all queryable wiki pages. Removing that scan entirely would likely require a broader source identity index or compile-metadata reuse path, so I am leaving that as a follow-up unless maintainers prefer it included before merge.

## Version context

The issue was reported against OpenClaw 2026.4.11, but this fix is developed against current `main`.
